meta.description: Day count convention tests validated against QuantLib output. Covers ISDA 2006 Definitions edge cases for 30/360 US (Bond Basis), 30E/360 (Eurobond), Act/Act ISDA, and Act/365L (AFB).

reference_source.name: QuantLib
reference_source.notes: Values computed using QuantLib::Thirty360, QuantLib::ActualActual, and QuantLib::Actual365Fixed conventions

cases.description: 30/360 US: Feb 28 (last day of Feb in non-leap year) to Mar 31. ISDA 4.16(f) EOM rule applies.
30/360 US: Feb 29 (last day of Feb in leap year) to Mar 31. ISDA 4.16(f) EOM rule applies.
cases.notes: Feb 29 (EOM in leap year) -> D1=30, Mar 31 -> D2=30. Days = 30

cases.description: 30/360 US: Feb 27 (NOT last day of Feb) to Mar 31. EOM rule does NOT apply.
30/360 US: Feb 28 to Feb 28 (next year), both EOM. Should equal exactly 1.0 year.
cases.notes: Both dates are last day of Feb -> D1=30, D2=30. Full year = 360 days

cases.description: 30/360 US: Feb 29 (leap) to Feb 28 (non-leap next year). Both are EOM.
cases.notes: Feb 29 (EOM leap) to Feb 28 (EOM non-leap) -> both D=30. Full year

cases.description: 30/360 US: Jan 31 to Feb 28. D1=31 triggers adjustment, but D2 is Feb EOM.
cases.notes: Jan 31 -> D1=30 (31 rule). Feb 28 EOM, but D1 was not Feb EOM, so D2=28. Days = 28-30+30 = 28

cases.description: Key test showing difference between 30/360 US and 30E/360: Jan 15 to Mar 31.
cases.notes: US: D1=15, D2=31 (D1_adj!=30) -> 76 days. Euro: D1=15, D2=30 (always adjust 31) -> 75 days

cases.description: 30E/360: Feb 28 is NOT adjusted (no EOM rule in European convention).
cases.notes: Euro: Feb 28 -> D1=28 (no EOM rule), Mar 31 -> D2=30. Days = 30-28+30 = 32

cases.description: Act/Act ISDA: Period spanning leap year boundary (Jul 1, 2024 to Jan 1, 2026).
Act/Act ISDA: Period entirely within leap year.
Act/Act ISDA: Period entirely within non-leap year.
Act/365L: Period containing Feb 29 uses 366 denominator.
cases.notes: 60 actual days, Feb 29 in range -> 60/366

cases.description: Act/365L: Period NOT containing Feb 29 uses 365 denominator.
cases.notes: 92 actual days, no Feb 29 in range -> 92/365

cases.description: Act/365L: Period in leap year but before Feb 29 uses 365.
cases.notes: 58 actual days, Feb 29 NOT in [Jan 1, Feb 28] -> 58/365

cases.description: Act/360: Standard money market convention.
cases.notes: 90 actual days / 360 = 0.25

cases.description: Act/365 Fixed: GBP money market convention.
cases.notes: 365 actual days / 365 = 1.0 (always uses 365 denominator)

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Full semi-annual coupon period. ICMA Rule 251.
cases.notes: Full 6-month period with semi-annual frequency = 0.5 year fraction exactly

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Full quarterly coupon period.
cases.notes: Full 3-month period with quarterly frequency = 0.25 year fraction exactly

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Full annual coupon period.
cases.notes: Full 1-year period with annual frequency = 1.0 year fraction exactly

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Short front stub (irregular first period). Common in bond markets.
cases.notes: 4 months in 6-month coupon period Mar 15 - Sep 15. 122 days / 184 days * 0.5 = 0.33152...

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Long first coupon spanning multiple quasi-periods.
cases.notes: Two full semi-annual periods = 2 × 0.5 = 1.0

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Period spanning leap year with semi-annual frequency.
cases.notes: Full semi-annual period in leap year still equals 0.5 (ICMA uses coupon-period basis)

cases.description: Act/Act ICMA: Monthly frequency (less common but valid).
cases.notes: Full monthly period = 1/12 ≈ 0.0833
